intro:
Badoo is one of the world's largest social discovery platforms with over 400 million users. Its reach is undeniable — particularly in Europe, Latin America, and Asia. But size comes with well-documented costs: fake profile rates, aggressive monetization, and a UX that prioritizes engagement over meaningful connection.

Embir targets the same international audience with a fundamentally different philosophy: verified profiles from day one, reporting and blocking at launch scale, compatibility-first matching across every orientation, and a model with free core connections with transparent freemium.

comparisonTable.competitor: Optional photo verification, easy to bypass
comparisonTable.embir: optional selfie verification with a visible badge + human review

comparisonTable.competitor: Free but heavily monetized, many paywalls
comparisonTable.embir: Genuinely free core features at launch
Universal from day one, all orientations
Reporting and blocking workflow, fast response

sections.title: Global reach vs meaningful connection
sections.content:
Badoo's scale is real — you'll find profiles in almost any city in the world. But a platform with 400M users that can't verify them is essentially a directory, not a dating experience. The noise-to-signal ratio is exhausting: expired profiles, bots, users who signed up once and never returned, and countless accounts that passed zero verification.

Embir's founding community is intentionally compact. Every member joined to participate — not to lurk, farm, or scam. Verification raises the floor. The trade-off is clear: fewer profiles, but dramatically higher quality and trust.
highlights: Smaller community = higher engagement and authenticity
Verified profiles only — no dead accounts cluttering discovery
Every report is recorded; blocking is immediate

sections.title: Monetization and free access
sections.content:
Badoo's monetization model is aggressive. 'Credits' to boost visibility, 'Superpower' subscription for basic features like seeing who liked you, in-app purchases for almost everything meaningful. The free tier is functional but frustrating — designed to push you toward spending.

Embir's core connection experience is genuinely free. Core discovery, messaging, and compatibility features work without a credit card. the optional-services model is announced up front: it will fund safety infrastructure and reporting and blocking, not gate essential human connection behind a paywall.
highlights: no credits, boosts or artificial urgency in core connections
Full messaging free — no 'send 5 messages then pay'

verdict:
Badoo is good for broad social discovery across markets where alternatives are thin. If you're in a major European or Latin American city and want volume, it delivers.

Embir is the better choice if you want verified humans, real compatibility matching, LGBTQ+ inclusion, and a free experience that doesn't constantly try to upsell you.
ctaText: Quality over quantity — verified dating, core connection features are free

faq.q: Is Embir available internationally like Badoo?
faq.a: Embir is currently focused on France, the UK, and the USA for launch density. International expansion is planned. The founding community period is the best time to join and help shape which markets grow first.

faq.q: How does Embir prevent fake profiles?
faq.a: selfie verification is optional and a reporting workflow records every report. reporting and blocking processes are designed to grow with the community.
Yes. Core connection features are free for everyone. No credits, no subscription, no hidden charges to message someone.

faq.q: Does Embir work for same-sex dating?
faq.a: Absolutely. Embir was built for every orientation — gay, lesbian, bi, pan, queer, trans, non-binary. Same-sex matching works the same way as any other pairing.
Embir is currently available in English and French. Additional languages will be added as the platform expands to new markets.
